refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a key performance indicator (KPI) requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement adds explicit reference to measurement/monitoring (telemetry) and duty cycle context to make verification concrete. High safety criticality tailoring requires that cycle time optimization does not compromise safety interlocks or emergency response.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under representative duty cycle; p95 cycle time computed from telemetry logs; comparison against CT_target acceptance threshold; safety event correlation analysis to ensure no safety compromise.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall introduce latency and control overhead not exceeding allocated cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that degrade cycle time below target, except during safety-critical interventions.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report cycle time metrics via telemetry for performance tracking.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/mechanical actuation timing; minimize travel path via route planning; parallelize load/dump operations where safe.
DP-B: Implement predictive cycle time modeling with real-time adjustment of operational parameters (speed, acceleration profiles) based on site conditions and duty cycle.
DP-C: Establish teleoperation control architecture with low-latency network link and deterministic command processing; implement safety-aware task scheduling.
DP-D: Integrate cycle time KPI monitoring into telemetry pipeline with continuous p95 aggregation and alerting on deviation from CT_target.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B + DP-C + DP-D (integrated): Implement a cycle time management system combining (1) predictive modeling and real-time parameter adjustment in the vehicle control layer, (2) low-latency teleoperation with safety-aware task scheduling, and (3) continuous telemetry-based KPI monitoring with p95 aggregation and alerting. This approach addresses both mechanical optimization (vehicle) and operational/monitoring layers (teleoperation, telemetry) required to achieve and sustain CT_target under variable site conditions.

potential_sources: Vehicle actuation timing (DP-B mechanical) is decoupled from teleoperation latency (DP-C network) via deterministic local control loops; teleoperation provides supervisory commands, not real-time servo control.
Safety services (DP-C safety-aware scheduling) are decoupled from cycle time optimization via explicit safety-first priority; safety interventions are allowed to exceed cycle time budget.
Telemetry monitoring (DP-D) is a passive observer; it does not feed back into real-time control, avoiding feedback coupling.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is minimized by clear separation of concerns: vehicle handles deterministic sub-cycle timing, teleoperation handles command latency and task scheduling, safety services enforce hard constraints, telemetry provides observability. No circular dependencies detected.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: CT_target is achievable within vehicle design constraints (hydraulic response time, mechanical inertia, operator skill level).
ASSUMPTION: Network link latency is predictable and bounded; teleoperation does not introduce unbounded jitter.
ASSUMPTION: Site conditions (terrain, load mass, ambient temperature) remain within duty cycle envelope; outlier conditions may violate CT_target.
RISK: Predictive modeling (DP-B) requires accurate site/load characterization; poor model fidelity will degrade cycle time optimization.
RISK: Safety-aware task scheduling (DP-C) may introduce conservative delays if safety logic is overly cautious; requires tuning to balance safety and productivity.
RISK: Telemetry aggregation (DP-D) may lag real-time performance; p95 computed from historical data may not reflect current site conditions.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-B to explicitly define the predictive model inputs (load mass, terrain type, operator skill level) and update frequency. Refine DP-C to specify the safety-aware task scheduling algorithm and priority rules. Refine DP-D to define the telemetry sampling rate, p95 window size, and alerting thresholds. Add explicit acceptance criteria for model accuracy and scheduling latency.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. At this decomposition level, the IM assignment reflects that cycle time KPI management is a system-level concern requiring coordination across all three sub-modules. The NGHE Solution module owns the cycle time requirement and orchestrates the integrated DP (predictive modeling, teleoperation control, safety scheduling, and telemetry monitoring).

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution outputs cycle time metrics (p95, mean, variance) and performance alerts to external monitoring systems (Site Manager dashboard, Project Manager reporting). Telemetry includes raw cycle time samples and aggregated KPI values.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task commands (load, travel, dump) from Site Manager or Tele-operator via Tasking interface. Task commands may include priority, deadline, or site-specific constraints that inform cycle time optimization.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution exchanges safety alerts with Safety Services. Outbound: cycle time optimization parameters that may affect safety margins. Inbound: safety constraints and emergency stop signals that override cycle time targets.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link for teleoperation command/feedback and telemetry transmission. Latency and bandwidth of Network Link directly impact cycle time performance and must be characterized.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integrator and owns the cycle time KPI requirement. Predictive modeling, teleoperation control, safety scheduling, and telemetry monitoring are all sub-functions that must be coordinated at the NGHE Solution level. Minimal diffusion: cycle time logic is localized to this module and its sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services).

Vehicle module owns the mechanical/hydraulic actuation and deterministic sub-cycle timing (load, travel, dump). However, cycle time optimization requires feedback from telemetry and coordination with teleoperation; this creates diffusion across module boundaries. Vehicle alone cannot achieve CT_target without teleoperation and monitoring.

Teleoperation module owns command latency and task scheduling logic. However, cycle time performance depends on vehicle actuation timing and safety constraints; teleoperation cannot independently optimize cycle time. Diffusion is moderate due to cross-module dependencies.

Safety Services owns safety-aware task scheduling and emergency interventions. However, safety constraints are reactive (triggered by hazard detection) and do not directly drive cycle time optimization. High diffusion: safety logic is distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Cycle time KPI management is a system-level concern that requires integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate owner because it has visibility into all three sub-modules and can coordinate predictive modeling, teleoperation control, safety scheduling, and telemetry monitoring. Containment is high (95%) because the cycle time logic is localized to the NGHE Solution and its sub-modules; diffusion is minimal (5%) because external dependencies (Network Link, Tasking, SafetyAlert) are well-defined interfaces.
containment_risks: RISK: Predictive modeling (DP-B) requires real-time data from Vehicle (load mass, terrain type) and Teleoperation (operator skill level, command latency). If Vehicle or Teleoperation modules do not expose these data via well-defined interfaces, the NGHE Solution will struggle to implement the model. Mitigation: define explicit data interfaces (e.g., Vehicle→NGHE: load_mass, terrain_type; Teleoperation→NGHE: operator_skill_level, command_latency).

RISK: Safety Services may impose hard constraints (e.g., maximum speed, mandatory safety checks) that conflict with cycle time optimization. If Safety Services is not tightly integrated with NGHE Solution, safety constraints may be applied reactively (after cycle time is already degraded). Mitigation: define explicit safety constraint interfaces (e.g., SafetyAlert→NGHE: max_speed, mandatory_check_duration) and incorporate them into predictive modeling.

RISK: Telemetry monitoring (DP-D) requires high-frequency sampling and low-latency aggregation. If Telemetry interface is not designed for real-time KPI computation, p95 metrics may lag actual performance. Mitigation: define explicit telemetry schema and sampling rate (e.g., cycle time sample every 100 ms, p95 aggregation window 1 hour).
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine the NGHE Solution module boundary to explicitly define sub-module interfaces for cycle time management: (1) Vehicle→NGHE: load_mass, terrain_type, sub_cycle_timing; (2) Teleoperation→NGHE: operator_skill_level, command_latency, task_priority; (3) Safety Services→NGHE: max_speed, mandatory_check_duration, safety_constraints; (4) NGHE→Telemetry: cycle_time_samples, p95_kpi, performance_alerts. Add a cycle time management function (e.g., CycleTimeOptimizer) as an explicit sub-module within NGHE Solution to own the predictive modeling and real-time parameter adjustment logic.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (integrated predictive modeling, teleoperation control, safety scheduling, and telemetry monitoring) directly addresses the refined FR (achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target with continuous monitoring). The DP is sufficiently detailed to guide design and is not over-constrained. Coupling analysis shows minimal circular dependencies. Assumptions and risks are documented and manageable.

diffusion_risks: Predictive modeling (DP-B) requires data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules; if these modules do not expose the required data via well-defined interfaces, diffusion will increase. Mitigation: define explicit data interfaces during next decomposition level.

Safety-aware task scheduling (DP-C) requires coordination with Safety Services; if safety constraints are not clearly communicated, scheduling logic will be scattered across modules. Mitigation: define explicit safety constraint interfaces.

Telemetry monitoring (DP-D) requires high-frequency data from Vehicle and Teleoperation; if telemetry infrastructure is not designed for real-time aggregation, monitoring logic will be diffused. Mitigation: define explicit telemetry schema and sampling rate.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for cycle time KPI management. Containment score is high (95%) because the cycle time logic is localized to the NGHE Solution and its sub-modules. Diffusion is minimal (5%) because external dependencies are well-defined interfaces. However, the recommended revision (define explicit sub-module interfaces) is critical to maintain containment during detailed design.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the refined FR (achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target with continuous monitoring) is feasible via instrumented field trials. Telemetry interface provides the necessary data (cycle time samples, p95 metrics) for verification. Acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target under agreed duty cycle) are measurable and objective.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The refined FR is clearly allocated to the NGHE Solution module, which owns the cycle time KPI requirement. Sub-FRs are allocated to Vehicle (deterministic sub-cycle timing), Teleoperation (low-latency control), Safety Services (safety-aware scheduling), and NGHE Solution (telemetry monitoring). Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-B to explicitly define the predictive model inputs, update frequency, and accuracy acceptance criteria.
Refine DP-C to specify the safety-aware task scheduling algorithm, priority rules, and latency acceptance criteria.
Refine DP-D to define the telemetry sampling rate, p95 window size, alerting thresholds, and data retention policy.
Define explicit sub-module interfaces for cycle time management (Vehicle→NGHE, Teleoperation→NGHE, Safety Services→NGHE, NGHE→Telemetry).
Add a cycle time management function (e.g., CycleTimeOptimizer) as an explicit sub-module within NGHE Solution to own the predictive modeling and real-time parameter adjustment logic.
Establish acceptance criteria for model accuracy, scheduling latency, and telemetry aggregation delay.

decision.justification: The refined FR, selected DP, and assigned IM pass all gate reviews. The cycle time KPI requirement is well-understood, the integrated DP is sound, and the NGHE Solution module is the appropriate owner. However, the DP is complex and requires decomposition into sub-functions (predictive modeling, teleoperation control, safety scheduling, telemetry monitoring) to guide detailed design. Decomposition is necessary to allocate cycle time budget across sub-modules, define sub-module interfaces, and manage diffusion risks. Proceeding to the next decomposition level is justified.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall introduce latency and control overhead not exceeding allocated cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that degrade cycle time below target, except during safety-critical interventions.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report cycle time metrics via telemetry for performance tracking.

assumptions: CT_target is achievable within vehicle design constraints (hydraulic response time, mechanical inertia, operator skill level).
Network link latency is predictable and bounded; teleoperation does not introduce unbounded jitter.
Site conditions (terrain, load mass, ambient temperature) remain within duty cycle envelope; outlier conditions may violate CT_target.
Predictive modeling can be implemented with acceptable accuracy using available site/load characterization data.
Safety-aware task scheduling can be tuned to balance safety and productivity without excessive conservative delays.
Telemetry infrastructure can support real-time p95 aggregation with acceptable latency and data retention.
open_risks: Predictive modeling (DP-B) requires accurate site/load characterization; poor model fidelity will degrade cycle time optimization.
Safety-aware task scheduling (DP-C) may introduce conservative delays if safety logic is overly cautious; requires tuning to balance safety and productivity.
Telemetry aggregation (DP-D) may lag real-time performance; p95 computed from historical data may not reflect current site conditions.
Sub-module interfaces (Vehicle→NGHE, Teleoperation→NGHE, Safety Services→NGHE) are not yet defined; diffusion risks may increase during detailed design.
Cycle time budget allocation across sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) is not yet established; may lead to over-allocation or under-allocation.
evidence_needed: Vehicle sub-cycle timing characterization (load, travel, dump durations under representative duty cycle).
Teleoperation latency and jitter characterization (command transmission, feedback reception, operator reaction time).
Safety constraint characterization (mandatory check durations, speed limits, emergency stop response time).
Site condition variability analysis (terrain types, load mass range, ambient temperature range).
Predictive model validation data (field trials with instrumented vehicle and telemetry logging).
Telemetry infrastructure performance characterization (sampling rate, aggregation latency, data retention capacity).
